---
title: "Inside Toronto's Shelter System"
subtitle: "Daily occupancy and monthly flow data from the City of Toronto — open, interactive, validated"
description: "Toronto shelter system data — daily occupancy and capacity, monthly system flow, validated against the City of Toronto's open data. Interactive tables with date selectors."
author: "Miriam Marling"
open-graph:
  title: "Inside Toronto's Shelter System"
  description: "Toronto shelter system data — daily occupancy and capacity, monthly system flow, validated against the City of Toronto's open data. Interactive tables with date selectors."
  site_name: "BonQuery — Data for the Greater Good"
twitter-card:
  card-style: summary
  title: "Inside Toronto's Shelter System"
  description: "Toronto shelter system data — daily occupancy and capacity, monthly system flow, validated against the City of Toronto's open data. Interactive tables with date selectors."
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
daily_occ = FileAttachment("../data/daily_occupancy.json").json()
```

This page is a hub for Toronto shelter system datasets sourced from the City of Toronto's open data portal. Each section covers one dataset — use the headings to jump to what you need.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Monthly Shelter System Flow

### The dashboards

::: {.mobile-dashboard-links}
Open each dashboard directly in your browser — they're built for
interactive use and work best full-screen on a phone.

- [Monthly Snapshot →](monthly-snapshot.html)
- [Year-to-Date Comparison →](ytd-comparison.html)
- [Historical Trends →](historical-trends.html)
:::

::: {.desktop-dashboard-tabs}

The three tabs below show different views of the data, with interactive
filters on every chart.

::: {.panel-tabset}

#### Monthly Snapshot

A point-in-time view of the shelter system for any month from January
2018 forward. Use the date filter at the top to change months.

[Open in a new tab →](monthly-snapshot.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="monthly-snapshot.html"
  width="100%"
  height="2800"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

#### Year-to-Date Comparison

Compares the current year's cumulative figures against the same period
the prior year. Useful for seeing whether the system is on track to
exceed, match, or fall below last year.

[Open in a new tab →](ytd-comparison.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="ytd-comparison.html"
  width="100%"
  height="900"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

#### Historical Trends

Multi-year time series for the headline metrics, with filters for
population group, year, and month. Useful for spotting long-term
patterns or seasonal trends.

[Open in a new tab →](historical-trends.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="historical-trends.html"
  width="100%"
  height="3800"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

:::

:::

```{=html}
<style>
.mobile-dashboard-links { display: none !important; }
.desktop-dashboard-tabs { display: block; }
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  .desktop-dashboard-tabs iframe {
    width: 100vw !important;
    margin-left: calc(50% - 50vw) !important;
    border: none !important;
    border-radius: 0 !important;
  }
}
</style>
```

:::

---

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Daily Occupancy & Capacity"}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// ── Date utilities ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function fmtDate(s) {
  const [y, m, d] = s.split("-").map(Number)
  return new Date(y, m - 1, d).toLocaleDateString("en-US", {
    year: "numeric", month: "long", day: "numeric"
  })
}

allDates = [...new Set(daily_occ.map(r => r.date))].sort().reverse()
latestDate = allDates[0]
// cityValidation = FileAttachment("../data/city_validation.json").json().catch(() => null)
// ^ Banner hidden — re-enable after capacity reconciliation is complete
```

BonQuery's version of this table adds two features the City's page doesn't have. The **date selector** below lets you browse any date from January 2021 to the present; the City's [Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Usage page](https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-census/){target="_blank"} shows only its most recently published date. The **Offline** column (calculated as `funded − actual`) flags beds, rooms, or spaces temporarily out of service — see the [data validation page](data-validation.html){target="_blank"} for what these are and why they matter.

## Daily Occupancy & Capacity for ${fmtDate(selectedDate)}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// ── Pipeline validation (CKAN-to-CKAN) — runs on most recent date ─────────
// These checks confirm BonQuery's aggregation pipeline is internally correct.
// They do not compare against the City's live dashboard.

latestRows = daily_occ.filter(r => r.date === latestDate)

function getVal(rows, key) {
  const row = rows.find(r => r.key === key)
  return row ? row.ind : null
}

validationChecks = {
  const rows = latestRows
  const checks = []

  // Check 1: all expected keys present
  const expectedKeys = [
    "all_shelter","room_based","singles_sector","singles_shelter","allied_summ",
    "temp_summ","iso_summ","fam_total","fam_emerg","fam_trans_r","fam_hotel",
    "sng_hotel","singles_total","emerg_total","mix_emerg","men_emerg","wom_emerg",
    "yth_emerg","trans_total","mix_trans","fam_trans_b","men_trans","wom_trans",
    "yth_trans","allied_total","respites","dropin","temp_resp","hotels","iso"
  ]
  const presentKeys = new Set(rows.map(r => r.key))
  const missingKeys = expectedKeys.filter(k => !presentKeys.has(k))
  checks.push({
    name: "All expected rows present",
    pass: missingKeys.length === 0,
    detail: missingKeys.length > 0 ? `Missing: ${missingKeys.join(", ")}` : null
  })

  // Check 2: All Shelter Programs total = sum of components
  const allShelter = getVal(rows, "all_shelter")
  const compSum = ["fam_total","sng_hotel","singles_total","allied_total","temp_resp","hotels","iso"]
    .reduce((s, k) => s + (getVal(rows, k) ?? 0), 0)
  checks.push({
    name: "All Shelter Programs rollup matches components",
    pass: allShelter === compSum,
    detail: allShelter !== compSum
      ? `all_shelter.ind=${allShelter}, component sum=${compSum}, diff=${allShelter - compSum}`
      : null
  })

  // Check 3: Singles sectors total = emergency + transitional
  const singlesTotal = getVal(rows, "singles_total")
  const emergTotal = getVal(rows, "emerg_total")
  const transTotal = getVal(rows, "trans_total")
  checks.push({
    name: "Singles sectors rollup: emergency + transitional = singles total",
    pass: singlesTotal === (emergTotal + transTotal),
    detail: singlesTotal !== (emergTotal + transTotal)
      ? `singles=${singlesTotal}, emerg+trans=${emergTotal + transTotal}`
      : null
  })

  // Check 4: For every row with non-null cap, occ + unocc = cap (within 1, for rounding)
  const capRows = rows.filter(r => r.cap !== null && r.cap > 0)
  const arithFails = capRows.filter(r => Math.abs(r.occ + r.unocc - r.cap) > 0.5)
  checks.push({
    name: "Internal arithmetic: occupied + unoccupied = actual capacity",
    pass: arithFails.length === 0,
    detail: arithFails.length > 0
      ? arithFails.map(r => `${r.key}: ${r.occ}+${r.unocc}≠${r.cap}`).join("; ")
      : null
  })

  return checks
}

allPass = validationChecks.every(c => c.pass)

// ── Validation banner ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
validationBanner = {
  const CITY_CENSUS_URL = "https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-census/"

  if (!allPass) {
    const failedChecks = validationChecks.filter(c => !c.pass)
    return html`<div style="
      background:#fff3cd;border:1px solid #e0a800;border-radius:4px;
      padding:8px 14px;font-size:0.88em;color:#533f03;margin-bottom:12px">
      <strong>⚠ Validation issue detected for ${fmtDate(latestDate)}</strong>
      <details style="margin-top:6px">
        <summary style="cursor:pointer">Show details</summary>
        <ul style="margin:6px 0 0;padding-left:20px">
          ${failedChecks.map(c => html`<li>${c.name}${c.detail ? html` — <em>${c.detail}</em>` : ""}</li>`)}
        </ul>
      </details>
    </div>`
  }
  // ── City-vs-BonQuery banner hidden ────────────────────────────────────────
  // Bridging & Triage (29 ppl) and known capacity differences cause the
  // current validator to always fail. Re-enable after the capacity
  // reconciliation analysis so we can show a clean ✓ / * / status that
  // distinguishes documented structural differences from genuine discrepancies.
  return html``
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof selectedDate = Inputs.select(allDates, {
  label: "Date",
  format: d => fmtDate(d)
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// ── Table rendering ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dayRows = daily_occ.filter(r => r.date === selectedDate)

function n(v) { return v !== null && v !== undefined ? v.toLocaleString() : "—" }
function pct(v) { return v !== null && v !== undefined ? v.toFixed(1) + "%" : "—" }

function offlineCell(r) {
  if (r.cap === null || r.cap === undefined) return `<td></td>`
  if (r.cap_funding === null || r.cap_funding === undefined) return `<td></td>`
  const offline = r.cap_funding - r.cap
  if (offline <= 0) {
    return `<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right">${offline.toLocaleString()}</td>`
  }
  return `<td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right;color:#cc0000;font-weight:700">${offline.toLocaleString()}</td>`
}

function tableRow(r, col1Label) {
  const pad = (r.indent ?? 0) * 16
  const bold = r.is_total ? "font-weight:600" : ""
  const bg   = r.is_total ? "#e8edf5" : "#ffffff"
  if (r.col_type === "summary") {
    return `<tr style="background:${bg}">
      <td style="padding:5px 10px 5px ${pad}px;${bold}">${r.label}</td>
      <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right;${bold}">${n(r.ind)}</td>
      <td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
    </tr>`
  }
  return `<tr style="background:${bg}">
    <td style="padding:5px 10px 5px ${pad}px;${bold}">${r.label}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right;${bold}">${n(r.ind)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right">${n(r.occ)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right">${n(r.unocc)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right">${n(r.cap)}</td>
    ${offlineCell(r)}
    <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right">${pct(r.rate)}</td>
  </tr>`
}

function sectionHeader(label, col1, col2, col3, col4) {
  return `<tr style="background:#1a3c6b;color:white;font-weight:600;font-size:0.85em">
    <td style="padding:7px 10px">${label}</td>
    <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Individuals</td>
    <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">${col1}</td>
    <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">${col2}</td>
    <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">${col3}</td>
    <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">${col4}</td>
    <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Occupancy Rate</td>
  </tr>`
}

function summaryHeader() {
  return `<tr style="background:#1a3c6b;color:white;font-weight:600;font-size:0.85em">
    <td style="padding:7px 10px">Occupancy Summary</td>
    <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Individuals</td>
    <td colspan="5"></td>
  </tr>`
}

dailyTable = {
  const s = (key) => dayRows.find(r => r.key === key)

  // Two rows the City shows at the top of the summary section.
  // "Total People Accommodated" = our All Shelter total (Bridging & Triage excluded).
  // "Bridging & Triage" is not published to CKAN open data — shown greyed.
  const allShelterInd = s("all_shelter") ? s("all_shelter").ind : null
  const totalAccomRow = `<tr style="background:#e8edf5;font-weight:600">
    <td style="padding:5px 10px">Total People Accommodated †</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right">${n(allShelterInd)}</td>
    <td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
  </tr>`
  const bridgingRow = `<tr style="background:#f5f5f5;color:#999;font-style:italic">
    <td style="padding:5px 10px 5px 26px">Bridging &amp; Triage Programs</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 10px;text-align:right;font-size:0.85em">Not published to open data</td>
    <td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td></td>
  </tr>`

  const summaryKeys = ["all_shelter","room_based","singles_sector","singles_shelter","allied_summ","temp_summ","iso_summ"]
  const roomKeys    = ["fam_total","fam_emerg","fam_trans_r","fam_hotel","sng_hotel"]
  const bedKeys     = ["singles_total","emerg_total","mix_emerg","men_emerg","wom_emerg","yth_emerg",
                       "trans_total","mix_trans","fam_trans_b","men_trans","wom_trans","yth_trans"]
  const alliedKeys  = ["allied_total","respites","dropin"]

  return html`
    <div style="overflow-x:auto;margin:0 0 1rem">
    <table style="border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:0.88em;font-family:'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif;min-width:620px">
      ${summaryHeader()}
      ${totalAccomRow}
      ${bridgingRow}
      ${summaryKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      ${sectionHeader("Shelter Programs, Room-Based", "Occupied Rooms", "Unoccupied Rooms", "Actual Room Capacity", "Offline Rooms")}
      ${roomKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      ${sectionHeader("Shelter Programs, Bed-Based", "Occupied Beds", "Unoccupied Beds", "Actual Bed Capacity", "Offline Beds")}
      ${bedKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      ${sectionHeader("Allied Services", "Occupied Spaces", "Unoccupied Spaces", "Actual Space Capacity", "Offline Spaces")}
      ${alliedKeys.map(k => s(k) ? tableRow(s(k)) : "").join("")}

      <tr style="background:#1a3c6b;color:white;font-weight:600;font-size:0.85em">
        <td style="padding:7px 10px">Temporary Programs, Bed/Space-Based</td>
        <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Individuals</td>
        <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Occupied Beds/Spaces</td>
        <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Unoccupied</td>
        <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Actual Capacity</td>
        <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Offline Beds/Spaces</td>
        <td style="padding:7px 10px;text-align:right">Occupancy Rate</td>
      </tr>
      ${s("temp_resp") ? tableRow(s("temp_resp")) : ""}

      ${sectionHeader("Temporary Programs, Room/Unit-Based", "Occupied Rooms", "Unoccupied Rooms", "Actual Room Capacity", "Offline Rooms")}
      ${s("hotels") ? tableRow(s("hotels")) : ""}

      ${sectionHeader("Temporary Isolation/Recovery Programs", "Occupied Rooms", "Unoccupied Rooms", "Actual Room Capacity", "Offline Rooms")}
      ${s("iso") ? tableRow(s("iso")) : ""}
    </table>
    <p style="font-size:0.8em;color:#666;margin:4px 0 0">
      † Total People Accommodated excludes Bridging &amp; Triage programs,
      which are tracked in the City's operational system but not published
      to open data.
    </p>
    </div>`
}
```

This table reproduces the City of Toronto's Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Usage report using the City's open data. It does not include Bridging & Triage programs, which are not published to open data. Some figures shown on the City's live dashboard cannot be reproduced from the CKAN export. A full side-by-side comparison is available on the [data validation page](data-validation.html){target="_blank"}.

Contains information licensed under the [Open Government Licence – Toronto](https://open.toronto.ca/open-data-licence/). Source: City of Toronto Open Data — [Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Occupancy & Capacity](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/daily-shelter-overnight-service-occupancy-capacity/). Aggregated by [BonQuery](https://bonquery.ca).

BonQuery's aggregated data is available for download from 2021 to present: [JSON](https://bonquery.ca/data/daily_occupancy.json) · [CSV](https://bonquery.ca/data/daily_occupancy.csv)

:::

---

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Historical Occupancy Spike Detection

These spike-detection charts flag days when Toronto's shelter system accommodated a lot more people than its seasonal pattern and long-run trend would've predicted, not merely days that happened to be busy. Spikes are identified by removing two predictable forces (seasonality and long-run trend) with Seasonal-Trend decomposition using LOESS (STL) and measuring what is left over.

The amber band marks moderate anomalies (2 MAD-SD above the expected value), the red band marks pronounced ones (3 MAD-SD). The analysis counts individuals accommodated rather than the occupancy rate, and both thresholds are calibrated against the system's own behaviour over the preceding two years. It is retrospective and descriptive, not predictive. Full methodology and caveats are on the linked page.

::: {.mobile-dashboard-links}
- [Historical Occupancy Spike Detection →](spike-detection.html)
:::

::: {.desktop-dashboard-tabs}

[Open in a new tab →](spike-detection.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="spike-detection.html"
  width="100%"
  height="2800"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

:::

Contains information licensed under the [Open Government Licence – Toronto](https://open.toronto.ca/open-data-licence/). Source: City of Toronto Open Data — [Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Occupancy & Capacity](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/daily-shelter-overnight-service-occupancy-capacity/). Analysis and charts by BonQuery.
:::

---

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Shelter System Requests for Referrals

Monthly average daily calls to Toronto's 24/7 shelter referral line — calls referred
to a shelter space, unmatched individual callers, and total calls handled — with
interactive date controls. Replicates and extends the City's
[Shelter System Requests for Referrals](https://www.toronto.ca/city-government/data-research-maps/research-reports/housing-and-homelessness-research-and-reports/shelter-system-requests-for-referrals/)
page. Data sourced from two separate CKAN resources (call wrap-up codes and service
queue); see the page for important caveats on combining the two series.

::: {.mobile-dashboard-links}
- [Shelter System Requests for Referrals →](central-intake.html)
:::

::: {.desktop-dashboard-tabs}

[Open in a new tab →](central-intake.html){target="_blank"}

<iframe
  src="central-intake.html"
  width="100%"
  height="4200"
  style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>

:::

Contains information licensed under the [Open Government Licence – Toronto](https://open.toronto.ca/open-data-licence/). Source: City of Toronto Open Data — [Central Intake Calls](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/central-intake-calls/). Analysis and charts by BonQuery.
:::
